paper_title: Rice Mutant and Gene Function Analysis

methods.description: Process of screening an ethyl methanesulfonate (EMS) mutant library, identifying complete male-sterile mutants, characterizing their vegetative and floral phenotypes, and performing genetic analysis (F1/F2 crosses, segregation analysis, chi-square test) to determine the inheritance pattern of the male sterility.

steps.description: Screen an ethyl methanesulfonate (EMS) mutant library derived from the indica rice Huanghuazhan (HHZ).
steps.searchable_summary: screen EMS mutagenized rice population

steps.description: Isolated two complete male-sterile mutants (HT5763 and H5524).
steps.searchable_summary: identify and isolate plants exhibiting complete male sterility

steps.description: Both mutants exhibited normal vegetative and floral development (Fig. 1A, B).
steps.searchable_summary: observe and record vegetative and floral phenotypes

steps.description: except for the anthers being smaller without pollen (Fig. 1C-F).
steps.searchable_summary: observe and record anther size and pollen presence

steps.description: Both mutant plants pollinated with the wild-type (WT) pollen exhibited normal seed set, indicating normal female fertility.
steps.searchable_summary: perform crosses between mutant plants (female) and WT plants (male)

steps.description: The F1 plants were completely fertile.
steps.searchable_summary: grow F1 generation plants from mutant x WT crosses and check fertility

steps.description: and the F2 progeny exhibited 3:1 segregation of fertile to sterile.
steps.searchable_summary: grow F2 generation plants from self-pollinated F1 plants and count fertile/sterile individuals

steps.description: [232:80 for HT5763 (χ² 3:1 ¼ 0.0339) and 224:61 for H5524 (χ² 3:1 ¼ 1.0344)], suggesting that the male sterile phenotype was controlled by a single recessive gene for both mutants.
steps.searchable_summary: perform chi-square test to analyze segregation ratio

methods.method_name: Causal Gene Identification via Bulk Sequencing and SIMM
methods.description: Method used to identify the specific gene and mutation responsible for the male sterile phenotype in isolated mutants by sequencing pooled DNA from sterile F2 individuals and analyzing the data with the Simultaneous Identification of Multiple Causal Mutations (SIMM) pipeline.

steps.description: 30 sterile individuals in each F2 population mentioned above were selected for bulk sequencing (Supplementary Table S1).
steps.searchable_summary: select F2 sterile individuals for bulk sequencing

steps.description: The sequenced data were analyzed using the Simultaneous Identification of Multiple Causal Mutations (SIMM) pipeline.
steps.searchable_summary: perform bulk sequencing

steps.description: The sequenced data were analyzed using the Simultaneous Identification of Multiple Causal Mutations (SIMM) pipeline, which identified two different mutation sites in LOC_Os04g39470 (OsMYB80).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sequencing data using SIMM pipeline to identify candidate causal mutations

steps.description: which identified two different mutation sites in LOC_Os04g39470 (OsMYB80).
steps.searchable_summary: pinpoint the specific genomic locations of identified mutations within the OsMYB80 gene

steps.description: The G to A mutation in HT5763 caused Glu 74 (GAG) substitution by Lys (AAG), and the T to A mutation in H5524 caused Trp 37 (TGG) substitution by Arg (AGG) (Fig. 1I).
steps.searchable_summary: identify the specific nucleotide substitution and predict the resulting amino acid change for each mutation
outputs: Amino acid change (e.g., Glu to Lys)

methods.description: Using High-resolution melting (HRM) analysis to confirm that the identified mutations in OsMYB80 co-segregate with the male sterile phenotype in F2 individuals.

steps.description: High-resolution melting (HRM) analysis of the F2 individuals indicated that both mutations co-segregated with the male sterile phenotype.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct HRM analysis on F2 individuals to genotype the OsMYB80 mutation

steps.description: High-resolution melting (HRM) analysis of the F2 individuals indicated that both mutations co-segregated with the male sterile phenotype.

steps.searchable_summary: compare the genotype data from HRM with the male sterile phenotype to confirm co-segregation

methods.description: A genetic experiment to confirm that the identified mutations in OsMYB80 are indeed responsible for the male sterile phenotype by introducing a wild-type copy of the gene into the mutant background.

steps.description: a complementation experiment was performed by introducing a 2,512-bp genomic DNA fragment, including 1,203-bp upstream region and 1,309-bp gene body of OsMYB80, into the HT5763 mutant plant.
steps.searchable_summary: construct a DNA fragment containing the OsMYB80 gene including upstream and gene body regions

steps.description: a complementation experiment was performed by introducing a 2,512-bp genomic DNA fragment, including 1,203-bp upstream region and 1,309-bp gene body of OsMYB80, into the HT5763 mutant plant.
steps.searchable_summary: transform the HT5763 mutant plants with the complementation construct

steps.description: Five independent transgenic lines of homozygous mutation background showed normal anther and pollen fertility (Fig. 1G, H).

steps.description: Five independent transgenic lines of homozygous mutation background showed normal anther and pollen fertility (Fig. 1G, H).

methods.description: Analyzing the tissue specificity and developmental expression patterns of the OsMYB80 gene using quantitative reverse transcription PCR (qRT-PCR).

steps.description: we analyzed the tissue specificity and developmental expression patterns of OsMYB80 using quantitative reverse transcription PCR (qRT-PCR). OsMYB80 was barely expressed in root, stem, leaf, lemma, palea, sterile lemma and pistil tissues. In anthers, however, the gene showed a developmentally regulated expression pattern (Fig. 2).
steps.searchable_summary: collect various rice tissues and anthers at different developmental stages

steps.description: using quantitative reverse transcription PCR (qRT-PCR).
steps.searchable_summary: extract RNA from collected tissue samples

steps.description: using quantitative reverse transcription PCR (qRT-PCR).
steps.searchable_summary: synthesize cDNA from RNA using reverse transcription

steps.description: using quantitative reverse transcription PCR (qRT-PCR).
steps.searchable_summary: perform quantitative reverse transcription PCR using OsMYB80 and reference gene primers
inputs: qRT-PCR primers (for OsMYB80 and reference gene)

steps.description: OsMYB80 was barely expressed in root, stem, leaf, lemma, palea, sterile lemma and pistil tissues. In anthers, however, the gene showed a developmentally regulated expression pattern (Fig. 2). OsMYB80 mRNA was very low in anthers before meiosis (stage 7), quickly increased during meiosis (stage 8), reached the highest level when microspores were released from the tetrad (stage 9), then declined to a very low level when microspores were vacuolated (stage 10) and became undetectable afterward (Fig. 2).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative gene expression levels and analyze expression patterns

methods.description: Performing DNA affinity purification sequencing (DAP-seq) to identify genomic DNA sequences bound by the OsMYB80 protein, including wet-lab procedures for library preparation and protein-DNA binding, and subsequent bioinformatics analysis for peak identification and annotation.

steps.description: Briefly, 5 μg HHZ genomic DNA was sheared into ~200-bp fragments.
steps.searchable_summary: fragment genomic DNA to a specific size (~200 bp)

steps.description: and then ligated with the Illumina-based sequencing adaptors to form a DNA library.
steps.searchable_summary: attach Illumina sequencing adaptors to DNA fragments

steps.description: The library was examined for adaptor-ligation frequency by quantitative PCR (qPCR) before applied to DAP-seq assay.
steps.searchable_summary: use qPCR to check the efficiency of adaptor ligation in the library

steps.description: OsMYB80 ORF fused to the Halo affinity tag was expressed in vitro using T N T V R SP6 Coupled Wheat Germ Extract System (Promega, Fitchburg, WI, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: produce the HaloTag-OsMYB80 fusion protein using an in vitro expression system

steps.description: The HaloTag-OsMYB80 protein was then purified from nonspecific proteins in the expression system using the magnetic HaloTag ligand (chloroalkane) beads (Promega).
steps.searchable_summary: isolate the fusion protein using magnetic HaloTag ligand beads

steps.description: and verified by Western blotting with the anti-HaloTag antibody (Promega).
steps.searchable_summary: confirm the presence and size of the purified protein using Western blot with an anti-HaloTag antibody

steps.description: The purified protein was incubated with 500 ng adaptor-ligated genomic DNA library at 30 C for 2 h.
steps.searchable_summary: mix purified protein with the adaptor-ligated DNA library to allow binding

steps.description: before washing away the unbound DNA fragments.
steps.searchable_summary: remove DNA fragments that did not bind to the protein using washes

steps.description: The samples were heated at 98 C for 10 min to release the OsMYB80-bound DNA.
steps.searchable_summary: elute the DNA fragments that were bound to the protein by heating

steps.description: and the recovered DNA was then PCR-amplified with the indexed TruSeq primers (Illumina, San Diego, CA, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: amplify the recovered DNA fragments using indexed primers for sequencing

steps.description: Indexed DNA samples were subsequently combined.
steps.searchable_summary: pool multiple indexed libraries for sequencing

steps.description: and size-selected to remove the residual adaptor dimers.
steps.searchable_summary: purify the combined library and remove adaptor dimers based on size
inputs: Size selection method (e.g., gel extraction, beads)

steps.description: Purified DNA libraries were then sequenced using the Illumina HiSeq sequencing platform.
steps.searchable_summary: perform high-throughput sequencing on the prepared library

steps.description: To identify OsMYB80-binding sequences, the clean DAP-seq reads were aligned to the Nipponbare reference genome (MSU v7.0) with HISAT2 (Kim et al. 2015).
steps.searchable_summary: map sequencing reads to the rice reference genome

steps.description: The enriched peaks were identified using MACS2 (Zhang et al. 2008b) with Àlog10(P-value) !5.
steps.searchable_summary: use MACS2 software to call peaks representing protein binding sites

steps.description: Then, the regions of the peaks were defined based on the annotations of the reference genome. Those located within 1.5 kb upstream of a gene were defined as peaks in promoters, and those located within 1 kb downstream of a gene were defined as peaks in 3 0 -UTR. Peaks located with >1.5 kb from the boundaries of a gene were defined as peaks in intergenic regions.
steps.searchable_summary: determine the genomic location of identified peaks relative to gene features
